guideline_references: National Bowel Cancer Screening Program (NBCSP) Guidelines
RACGP Guidelines for Preventive Activities in General Practice (Red Book) — colorectal cancer screening
RACGP / beyondblue guidance on grief, acute stress and situational anxiety
intentional_test_features: Benign-title / anxiety-framing red herring: the source note wraps a routine, asymptomatic population bowel-cancer screening counselling encounter in a dramatic 'severe panic attack / urgent face-to-face required' urgency justification. The scored entity is the BENIGN preventive-screening encounter with a self-limiting situational stress episode — the test is whether the system resists over-triaging on the anxiety framing and does NOT escalate a resolved panic to ED/000, while still surfacing the genuine cardiac and bowel red-flag safety-nets.

Telehealth reprojection: the source clinician performed a hands-on physical examination to reassure and physically opened the screening kit with the patient. The AI Doctor CANNOT perform the reassuring exam, cannot measure clinician vitals without a device, and cannot demonstrate the kit hands-on; the AI-correct action is remote reassurance + screening counselling + arranging routine in-person GP / practice-nurse support and bereavement follow-up at low acuity.

Over-triage-resistance test: the clinician-measured tachycardia (HR 112) and hypertension (BP 155/95) are sympathetic-arousal findings sealed in node 11; the AI must not read them as an acute cardiac emergency. Under-triage guard is preserved via explicit chest-pain (000) and bowel red-flag (GP) escalation edges.
Age inferred as older_adult (~64) from 'asymptomatic individual aged 50-74' + 'elderly patient' — flagged. Sex at birth not stated in source — inferred and flagged.
Source category: GPO (General Practice Other), source case GPO-028.
